_meta.purpose: Citation-ID -> human description map for Step 1 Triage. Per the architecture assessment, golden-copy references use STABLE citation IDs, never hardcoded source filenames (which would deepen filename coupling). This map deliberately does NOT point to source-*.md paths; a future citation index can resolve IDs -> files in one place.
_meta.convention: IDs are jurisdiction/law-anchored (e.g. SFL-ART-11 = State Finance Law Article 11). Non-golden-copy laws (PAL, GML, EDN) are included so jurisdiction-gate reasons can cite WHY a matter is out of the golden copy without inventing coverage.

citations.SFL-ART-11: State Finance Law Article 11 — State purchasing/contracting (the golden-copy-verified STATE-agency framework).
citations.SFL-163: State Finance Law §163 — Purchasing services and commodities.
citations.SFL-163-6: State Finance Law §163(6) — discretionary purchasing authority and thresholds (provisional in this build).
citations.SFL-163-10: State Finance Law §163(10) — single/sole-source and exemption determinations.
citations.PAL-2879: Public Authorities Law §2879 — public-authority procurement contract guidelines (NOT in the golden copy).
citations.GML: General Municipal Law — municipal (city/town/village/county/school district) bidding (NOT in the golden copy).
citations.EDN-SUNY-CUNY: Education Law — SUNY/CUNY procurement authority (coverage unverified; NOT in the golden copy).
